climate-treaty.title: International Convention on Climate Accountability and Atmospheric Restoration

preamble.text: Recalling the Paris Agreement adopted under the United Nations Framework Convention on Climate Change on 12 December 2015

Noting with grave concern the findings of the Intergovernmental Panel on Climate Change Sixth Assessment Report indicating that global temperatures have already exceeded 1.1°C above pre-industrial levels
Recognizing the principle of common but differentiated responsibilities and respective capabilities
Deeply concerned by the disproportionate impact of climate change on least developed countries and small island developing states

operativeClauses.text: Decides to establish a binding emissions reduction framework requiring all Parties to reduce greenhouse gas emissions by 45 percent below 2019 levels by 2035
Establishes an International Climate Accountability Mechanism with authority to monitor, verify, and certify national emissions inventories through independent satellite-based observation systems
Decides that Parties exceeding allocated carbon budgets shall contribute to the Loss and Damage Fund at a rate proportional to excess emissions
Calls upon developed country Parties to provide no less than 200 billion USD annually in climate finance to developing countries for adaptation and mitigation
Urges all Member States to phase out fossil fuel subsidies within five years and redirect funds toward renewable energy infrastructure
Requests the Secretary-General to report annually to the General Assembly on the implementation of the present resolution

ai-governance.title: Establishment of the International Agency for Artificial Intelligence Governance

preamble.text: Recalling General Assembly resolution 78/265 on seizing the opportunities of safe, secure and trustworthy artificial intelligence systems
Recognizing that frontier artificial intelligence systems pose novel risks to international peace and security, human rights, and economic stability
Affirming that the governance of artificial intelligence must be inclusive, transparent, and respect the sovereignty of all nations

operativeClauses.text: Decides to establish the International Agency for Artificial Intelligence (IAAI) as a specialized agency of the United Nations system
Mandates the IAAI to develop binding international standards for the safety testing and deployment of frontier AI systems exceeding defined capability thresholds
Authorizes the IAAI to conduct inspections of AI development facilities upon reasonable suspicion of non-compliance with safety standards
Calls upon all Member States to establish national AI governance bodies and report annually on domestic AI development activities
Requests the IAAI to maintain a registry of frontier AI systems and facilitate technology transfer to developing countries
Urges Member States to impose moratoriums on AI systems demonstrated to pose existential risk until adequate safeguards are verified

nuclear-ban.title: Convention on the Complete Elimination of Nuclear Weapons

preamble.text: Recalling the Treaty on the Non-Proliferation of Nuclear Weapons and the obligations of nuclear-weapon States under Article VI thereof
Reaffirming the Treaty on the Prohibition of Nuclear Weapons adopted on 7 July 2017
Deeply alarmed by the continued modernization of nuclear arsenals and the erosion of the arms control architecture
Convinced that the existence of nuclear weapons constitutes an existential threat to all humanity

operativeClauses.text: Decides that all States possessing nuclear weapons shall reduce their arsenals to zero warheads within fifteen years of entry into force of the present Convention
Establishes an International Verification Agency with unfettered access to all facilities associated with nuclear weapons programs
Demands that all States parties place fissile materials under international safeguards within three years
Provides security guarantees to disarming States through a collective security mechanism under the Security Council
Calls upon all States not party to this Convention to accede without preconditions
Decides that violations of this Convention shall be referred to the Security Council under Chapter VII of the Charter

sc-reform.title: Reform and Expansion of the Security Council

preamble.text: Recalling the ongoing intergovernmental negotiations on Security Council reform pursuant to decision 62/557
Recognizing that the current composition of the Security Council does not reflect contemporary geopolitical realities
Affirming the principle of equitable geographical representation in the principal organs of the United Nations

operativeClauses.text: Decides to expand the Security Council from fifteen to twenty-one members, adding six new permanent seats
Allocates new permanent seats as follows: two to Africa, two to Asia-Pacific, one to Latin America and the Caribbean, and one to Western Europe and Others
Decides that the veto power shall henceforth require two concurrent negative votes from permanent members to prevent adoption of a resolution
Requests all permanent members to justify any exercise of the veto before the General Assembly within ten days
Decides that these amendments to the Charter shall enter into force upon ratification by two thirds of the Members including all current permanent members

cyber-norms.title: Binding International Norms on State Conduct in Cyberspace

preamble.text: Recalling the reports of the Group of Governmental Experts on Developments in the Field of Information and Telecommunications in the Context of International Security
Gravely concerned by the increasing frequency and severity of state-sponsored cyber operations targeting civilian critical infrastructure
Affirming that international humanitarian law applies to cyber operations during armed conflict

operativeClauses.text: Decides that state-sponsored cyber operations targeting civilian critical infrastructure including hospitals, water systems, power grids, and financial networks constitute a threat to international peace and security
Demands that all States refrain from conducting or supporting cyber operations designed to damage or disable civilian infrastructure of another State
Establishes an International Cyber Attribution Panel with authority to investigate and publicly attribute significant cyber incidents
Decides that confirmed violations of these norms shall be subject to graduated sanctions under Article 41 of the Charter
Calls upon all States to establish national computer emergency response teams and participate in international information sharing mechanisms

water-rights.title: Universal Right to Clean Water and Sanitation: Binding Framework for Achievement by 2035

preamble.text: Recalling General Assembly resolution 64/292 recognizing the human right to water and sanitation
Noting with deep concern that 2.2 billion people still lack safely managed drinking water services
Reaffirming Sustainable Development Goal 6 to ensure availability and sustainable management of water and sanitation for all
Recognizing that the climate crisis is exacerbating water scarcity and threatening the human right to water

operativeClauses.text: Declares that access to clean water and adequate sanitation is a binding obligation of all States under international human rights law
Decides to establish a Global Water Access Fund capitalized at 50 billion USD to finance water infrastructure in least developed countries
Requires all States to submit national water access plans with measurable targets and annual progress reports
Urges States to prioritize water access over commercial exploitation in their water resource management policies
Calls upon international financial institutions to provide preferential lending for water and sanitation infrastructure
Requests the Secretary-General to appoint a Special Rapporteur on Universal Water Access with a mandate to investigate non-compliance

palestine-statehood.title: Admission of the State of Palestine as a Full Member of the United Nations

preamble.text: Recalling General Assembly resolution 67/19 according Palestine non-member observer State status
Reaffirming the right of the Palestinian people to self-determination and to their independent State
Noting that 145 United Nations Member States have recognized the State of Palestine

operativeClauses.text: Decides to admit the State of Palestine to membership in the United Nations with all rights and privileges thereof
Requests the Security Council to reconsider favourably the application of Palestine for membership
Affirms the territorial integrity of the State of Palestine on the basis of pre-1967 borders
Calls upon all States to recognize the State of Palestine and establish diplomatic relations
Demands Israel cease all settlement activities in the occupied Palestinian territory

debt-relief.title: Establishment of a Multilateral Sovereign Debt Restructuring Mechanism

preamble.text: Noting with deep concern that external debt servicing absorbs over 30 percent of government revenue in 54 developing countries
Recalling the Addis Ababa Action Agenda on financing for development
Recognizing the inadequacy of existing ad hoc approaches to sovereign debt resolution

operativeClauses.text: Decides to establish a binding multilateral framework for orderly sovereign debt restructuring
Requires all creditors including private bondholders to participate in restructuring proceedings
Establishes that debt service payments shall not exceed 15 percent of government revenue
Calls for reform of voting shares in the International Monetary Fund to reflect current economic realities
Urges developed countries to cancel bilateral debts of least developed countries

autonomous-weapons.title: Convention on the Prohibition of Lethal Autonomous Weapons Systems

preamble.text: Recalling the principles of international humanitarian law including the requirement for human judgment in the use of force
Alarmed by the rapid development of weapons systems capable of selecting and engaging targets without meaningful human control
Noting the Guiding Principles on Lethal Autonomous Weapons Systems adopted by the CCW High Contracting Parties

operativeClauses.text: Decides to prohibit the development, production, and use of weapons systems that can select and engage human targets without meaningful human control
Requires all States to maintain meaningful human control over critical functions of weapons systems
Establishes a verification regime including declaration of AI-enabled weapons research programmes
Calls upon States to impose a moratorium on deployment of autonomous weapons pending entry into force
Requests the Secretary-General to establish a scientific advisory panel on autonomous weapons technology

digital-rights.title: International Charter on Digital Rights and Freedoms

preamble.text: Reaffirming that the rights held by people offline must also be protected online
Deeply concerned by the expansion of mass digital surveillance programmes by States
Recognizing the digital divide as a barrier to the enjoyment of human rights

operativeClauses.text: Declares that all persons have the right to privacy in digital communications free from arbitrary surveillance
Calls upon States to ensure that surveillance measures are subject to independent judicial oversight
Urges States to guarantee access to the open internet as essential for the exercise of human rights
Requests the High Commissioner for Human Rights to report on State compliance with digital rights standards
Demands States refrain from internet shutdowns and disruption of access to information

climate-reparations.title: Binding Framework for Climate Reparations and Historical Emissions Liability

preamble.text: Recalling the principle of common but differentiated responsibilities and the historical contribution of developed countries to cumulative greenhouse gas emissions
Noting that the 20 largest historical emitters are responsible for over 80 percent of cumulative CO2 emissions
Gravely concerned by the Loss and Damage Fund remaining inadequately capitalized

operativeClauses.text: Decides that States with cumulative historical emissions exceeding defined thresholds bear legal liability for climate-related loss and damage
Establishes mandatory annual contributions to the Climate Reparations Fund proportional to historical emissions
Sets the initial capitalization target at 500 billion USD annually from 2027
Grants most vulnerable countries priority access to compensation for climate-related disasters
Requests the ICJ to provide an advisory opinion on the legal obligations of major emitters

space-governance.title: Treaty on the Prevention of an Arms Race in Outer Space

preamble.text: Recalling the Outer Space Treaty of 1967 and its prohibition on placing weapons of mass destruction in orbit
Deeply concerned by the development and testing of anti-satellite weapons by multiple States
Recognizing that the weaponization of space threatens all nations dependent on satellite infrastructure

operativeClauses.text: Prohibits the placement, testing, or use of any weapons system in outer space or directed against objects in outer space
Bans the development and testing of anti-satellite weapons systems
Establishes an International Space Verification Agency with monitoring capabilities
Requires States to register all objects launched into orbit and declare their function
Creates a liability regime for damage caused by space debris generated from weapons testing
